overview: The system is a minimal single-component web application that serves a "Hello World" HTML page directly from a Go HTTP server. A single backend service handles all routing, renders the HTML response, and applies required security headers — no separate frontend build pipeline or database is needed. The service is containerized via Docker and deployed on Kubernetes, exposing port 9090.
requirements: The application MUST display the text 'Hello World' prominently on the root path (/) without any user interaction.
The root path (/) MUST return HTTP 200 OK with a valid HTML document including <!DOCTYPE html>, <html>, <head> (with a <title>Hello World</title>), and <body> elements.
Any undefined/unknown path MUST return HTTP 404 Not Found with a user-friendly error page (no stack traces or internal details exposed).
The 'Hello World' heading MUST be horizontally centered and use a minimum base font size of 16px.
The page layout MUST be responsive across desktop (≥1024px), tablet (768px–1023px), and mobile (<768px) screen widths.
Total page asset size MUST not exceed 500 KB.
The application MUST respond to all HTTP requests within 500 ms under up to 100 concurrent users.
The application MUST set the following HTTP security headers on every response: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer.
The application MUST NOT expose server-side error messages, stack traces, or internal system information to end users.
A /health endpoint MUST return HTTP 200 OK with a JSON body {"status": "ok"} for liveness/readiness probes.
The application MUST serve content over HTTPS when deployed to a public-facing environment (handled at ingress/infrastructure layer).
The application MUST target 99.9% uptime during normal operation.

components.name: hello-world-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Service API
  description: Serves a Hello World HTML page and a health check endpoint.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns a fully rendered HTML page displaying 'Hello World' with responsive layout and security headers.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful HTML page response
          headers:
            X-Content-Type-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: nosniff
            X-Frame-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: DENY
            X-XSS-Protection:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: 1; mode=block
            Referrer-Policy:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: no-referrer
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: '<!DOCTYPE html><html>...</html>'
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns service health status for Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 HTML page for any undefined route.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions: ## Component: hello-world-service ### Language & Framework - **Language:** Go (latest stable, e.g. 1.22+) - **Framework:** Standard library only — `net/http` - **Port:** 9090 ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-service main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` ### go.mod - Module name: `hello-world-service` - No external dependencies — stdlib only. ### main.go — Behavior #### Security Headers Middleware Create a middleware function `securityHeaders(next http.Handler) http.Handler` that sets the following headers on **every** response before delegating: - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` - `X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block` - `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` #### Routes Use `http.NewServeMux()` and register: 1. **`GET /`** — Serve the Hello World HTML page (status 200, Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8). Use an inline Go template or raw string literal. The HTML must: - Include ``, ``, ``, ``. - `Hello World` in ``. - Inline `
